visa_types.overview:
Overview
This type of visa will be issued to applicants who wish to enter the Kingdom for the following purposes:
- to travel to Thailand for tourism purpose (category "TR")
- to attend medical treatment in short term not exceed than 60 days (category "MT")
General
Applicants must submit all documents in accordance with a certain checklist especially the employment background and educational documents of the non-B visa applicant.
Signature on the application form and the checklist match with the applicant's signature.
Hotel reservations
The rest of the stay in Thailand reservation documents should be a confirmation from the hotel and not from the travel agency.
Hotel reservation confirmation should be 1 page (PDF print in colour). Please refrain from submitting a hotel reservation printout from the email.
Check-in/out date is in sync with the date of travel.
The hotel reservation confirmation must be printed in colour.
Flight Tickets
Name of the applicant on the tickets is in accordance with the applicant's name.
The ticket should be confirmed and submit a colour copy of the e-ticket, the travel agent booking document will not be considered.
Bank Statement
The last transaction shown in the bank statement should not be older than 7 days from the date of submission.
Name and digits must be clear.
The bank statement must show the final amount for every transaction.
Bank statements should be attested by the bank, in case the statement has page numbers mentioned attestation on the first and last page, if the page numbers are missing attestation from the bank is required on all pages.
If the bank statement document is a copy of the bank passbook, it needs to be attested by the bank and the original passbook needs to be submitted with the visa application.
Bank statement with sufficient balance and amount maintenance of the applicant. It should be as mentioned on the visa application checklist for which the applicant has applied.
Others.
Family Visa- Submission of birth certificate and marriage certificate should be attested by MEA, the marriage certificate should be registered under the laws and no affidavits will be accepted, Parents visa should be valid and have a valid work visa. Original certificates need to be attached along with the application and to be returned together with the passport.
For Indian married with Thai national- Only marriage under Special Marriage Act or Thai law is accepted.
For family or dependent visas, cover letters should be submitted with valid IDs. Original cover letter from spouse or parents will only be accepted. This is to avoid a forged signature.
Employment Visa- Applicants need to submit all recommendation, education, and work experience documents.
In case of sponsorship, it should be from a direct family member (i.e. spouses, parents, children, siblings from the same parents) and proof of relationship needs to be attached. Example- If a sibling is a sponsor then their passport copy having the same parents' name as of applicant will stand as proof, in the case of parents, birth certificate or parents' name on the back of passport will stand as proof.
If the applicant is attaching a cover letter for something, they need to provide the evidence for the same.
Applicants are requested to submit original documents as mentioned in the checklist e.g. an original invitation letter from the company for non-B visa applicants.
Please note that for non-B visa applications, the company documents must be signed by the authorized person which shall be mentioned on the company affidavit. In case the authorized person has given the power to another person, a PoA must also be submitted.
Please make sure that all additional documents requested are submitted when re-submitting.
Please note that failure to follow the above mentioned guidelines may result in the delay of visa issuance.

## Visa fees and Logistic fees

[Table:Destination Thailand Visa]
- Applicant applying in Chennai, Cochin, Bengaluru & Hyderabad needs to pay INR500/- service charges in addition to the Visa Fees (Including all taxes)
Kindly Note: - 
- All the charges are non refundable, even if the application is refused or withdrawn.
- Applicants are requested to collect appropriate receipts for all the payments.

## Documents Required
Please prepare your documents according to requirement in visa checklists before submitting your application. The guidelines checklist listed on this site will help you prepare your documents as accurately as possible.
It is mandatory to submit checklist along with all visa application forms and supporting documents at the time of submission. Set of all documents required should be attached along with all applications.
DESTINATION THAILAND VISA
Download the checklist of Destination Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1X2g7FjoiviCOjqvCNYeux/d063cbf290533d9821719c70a2ba01e0/destination-thailand-visa.pdf)

## Photo Specifications

- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have a white background only.
- It should have a close-up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 60%–70% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clean.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks or creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera with a neutral expression, neither frowning nor smiling, with the mouth closed.
- It should be 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one.
- It is to be taken without headgear.
- Photographs may be taken with headgear on a case-by-case basis if the applicant wears headgear for religious reasons. The main facial features must be visible: chin, nose, eyes, and eyebrows must not be covered, and the forehead should be uncovered to show the shape of the face.
- It should be without hat or cap.
- It should be with the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned and color photocopies will not be accepted.

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Consulate General Chennai ; for the processing of each application is minimum 3 to 5 working days from the day it is submitted at The Royal Thai Consulate General Chennai
Please Note:
Please note that this is an indicative processing time only. Processing time may increase or decrease at the discretion of The Royal Thai Consulate General Chennai
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of the Consulate General of Japan, Chennai.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Transit Visa is issued to applicants who wish to enter Thailand for any of the following purposes: 
- Category TS (Transit): to travel in transit through Thailand in order to proceed to the country of destination or to re-enter the applicant's own country
- Category S (Sports): To participate in sports activities
- Category C (Conveyance): To the person in charge or crew of a conveyance coming to a port, station or area in Thailand

## Photo Specifications

- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have a white background only.
- It should have a close-up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 60%–70% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clean.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks or creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera with a neutral expression, neither frowning nor smiling, with the mouth closed.
- It should be 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one.
- It is to be taken without headgear.

- It should be without hat or cap.
- It should be with the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
